text:
Once upon a time, in the quiet town of Willow Creek, there was a garden gnome named Gus. Gus lived in a little red house hidden between thickets of laurel and evergreens. He was renowned throughout the town for his wisdom and his ability to solve the toughest gardening problems.

On a bright, humid Sunday, a neighbor named Edda approached Gus's window with a worried expression.

"Ach," Gus said softly. "What troubles you?"

"My rose bushes are wilting. I've moved them all to the sunniest spot. They're probably just getting used to the bright light."

Gus peered outside. Sure enough, the sun was shining down relentlessly. But then it struck him.

"Rose bushes don't like direct sunlight. You should put them someplace lower."

Edda looked thoughtful. "You're right. Thank you."

Every Sunday, Gus would host a small gathering of townsfolk who were as curious about nature as he was. They listened to his advice on
